findings.issue: Top-level import of '@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog' at line 7 without guarding. This is a static import, not a dynamic import, so it will throw in web/non-Tauri environments (browser-mode serve). All other Tauri APIs in the codebase (TitleBar, MenuBar) use try/catch with dynamic imports. This one does not.
findings.recommendation: Replace the top-level `import { open } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog'` with a dynamic `await import('@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog')` inside `handleOpenProject`, wrapped in try/catch — matching the pattern in TitleBar.tsx and MenuBar.tsx.

findings.issue: Nested `<button>` inside `<button>` at lines 33–67. The expand/collapse toggle button (lines 44–57) is rendered inside the outer session-select button. Nested interactive elements are invalid HTML (spec violation) and cause inconsistent behavior in WebKitGTK — the component comment in SessionItem.tsx explicitly notes this same crash risk and uses div+role='button' to avoid it.
findings.recommendation: Extract the expand/collapse chevron button out of the parent button. Use a flex row with the chevron as a sibling to the main session-select area, or apply the same div+role='button'+tabIndex={0} pattern used in SessionItem.tsx.

findings.issue: MemoryBrowserPanel is defined and exported from panels/index.ts but is never imported anywhere in the application. It is a dead component — it cannot be reached by any user action or navigation path.
findings.recommendation: Either wire MemoryBrowserPanel into a reachable panel/tab (e.g., as a tab in the bottom panel alongside the existing SidebarMemory compact view), or delete the file to avoid maintenance burden. CodeEditorPanel has the same problem — it is exported from panels/index.ts but never consumed.

findings.issue: Right panel tabs (Activity, Files, Review, Trajectory, Team) are plain buttons with no role='tab', no aria-selected, and no role='tablist' on the container. The bottom panel in AppShell.tsx correctly uses role='tablist', role='tab', and aria-selected. The right panel is inconsistent.
findings.recommendation: Add role='tablist' to the tab header div, role='tab' and aria-selected={rightPanelTab() === tab.id} to each tab button. This brings the right panel into parity with the bottom panel's accessibility pattern.

findings.issue: Right panel tab content uses `<Show>` for mounting/unmounting (lines 143–172) rather than display:none. This means every tab switch destroys and remounts the child components. The bottom panel in AppShell.tsx explicitly uses `display:none` with a comment 'use display:none instead of <Show> so xterm DOM stays alive'. The same concern applies to any stateful panel like TrajectoryInspector which maintains scroll position and filter state.
findings.recommendation: Switch the right panel content containers to use the display:none pattern from AppShell.tsx, keeping all panel DOMs alive to preserve local state on tab switch.

findings.issue: No minimum window width enforcement in the layout itself beyond the Tauri window config (minWidth: 640 in TitleBar.tsx handleNewWindow). When all panels are open (ActivityBar 52px + Sidebar 180-480px + RightPanel 250-600px), the center MainArea has `min-w-0` but no explicit minimum, so it can collapse to zero pixels without any responsive fallback or collapse behavior for very narrow windows.
findings.recommendation: Add a minimum pixel threshold to the MainArea container (e.g., min-w-[300px]) and consider auto-hiding the sidebar or right panel when the window is narrower than a threshold. Alternatively, add a responsive `useWindowSize` check that auto-collapses the sidebar when width < 800px.

findings.issue: View menu labels are misleading: 'Toggle Memory' maps to toggleBottomPanel (which toggles a 3-tab panel containing Memory, Terminal, and Output — not just memory). 'Toggle Activity' maps to toggleRightPanel (which is a multi-tab panel with Activity, Files, Review, Trajectory, Team). The labels don't match what actually gets revealed.
findings.recommendation: Rename to 'Toggle Bottom Panel' and 'Toggle Right Panel' respectively, or enumerate the tabs in the label (e.g., 'Toggle Memory / Terminal / Output'). These are also the canonical names used everywhere else in the codebase.

findings.issue: ActivityBar lacks a containing role='navigation' or aria-label on its root div. Screen readers cannot distinguish this icon rail from other content regions. The buttons have individual aria-label attributes, but there is no landmark role to group them.
findings.recommendation: Add role='navigation' aria-label='Activity Bar' to the root div element of ActivityBar.

findings.issue: Resize handlers add mousemove/mouseup listeners to document during drag but there is no protection against the drag ending outside the window (e.g., mouse released on an OS-level drag). If mouseup fires outside the browser viewport (common when dragging to screen edge), the document mousemove listener is never cleaned up, leaking an active listener that fires on every subsequent mouse movement.
findings.recommendation: Add a `mouseleave` listener on `document.documentElement` or handle `window.blur` to call `onUp()` — matching how VS Code and similar tools prevent this leak.

findings.issue: The bottom panel has a hardcoded `max-height: 600px` at line 109, but the `setBottomPanelHeight` clamp also uses 600 as maximum. If a user somehow sets the height to exactly 600px and the panel height reaches max-height, the resize handle still appears and allows dragging upward, but no change occurs (clamped). Minor but can cause user confusion.
findings.recommendation: Unify the max-height: the CSS max-height and the JS clamp should either both be a shared constant or the max-height CSS should be derived from the same token.

findings.issue: Session search (line 51-55) only filters by session name using a simple string includes. There is no debounce on the search input. With many sessions, every keystroke triggers a synchronous filter over the full list.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the search input setter with a debounce (e.g., 150ms) or use SolidJS createDebounce to avoid refiltering on every keystroke. The nucleo fuzzy-search already used by SelectListState elsewhere in the app could be applied here for better match quality.

findings.issue: The TitleBar uses `role='toolbar'` on its root div (line 91), but a toolbar role expects focusable items and arrow-key navigation (roving tabIndex). The buttons inside do not implement the roving tabIndex pattern expected by the ARIA toolbar role.
findings.recommendation: Either remove role='toolbar' and leave it as a plain div, or implement the roving tabIndex pattern with arrow key handling. Since this is a custom title bar, a generic nav region may be more appropriate.

findings.issue: Rename input at line 99 uses `onBlur={() => submitRename()}` which will submit on any focus loss, including when the user clicks the 'Cancel' button (which is not rendered in rename mode — it's in delete mode). However, if the rename input loses focus to an external element, the rename is committed without explicit user intent. There is no Escape-to-cancel-without-commit warning to users.
findings.recommendation: On blur, check if the new value differs from the original before submitting — if unchanged, just cancel the rename mode instead of calling onRename. This prevents an empty or unchanged rename from being submitted as a real update.

findings.issue: panels/index.ts exports CodeEditorPanel and MemoryBrowserPanel, but neither is imported anywhere in the application. Dead exports add to bundle size and create confusion about what is actually in use.
findings.recommendation: Remove the unused exports from the index barrel, or add a comment marking them as 'available but not yet wired' to differentiate intentional future components from accidental dead code.

findings.issue: The bottom panel 'Memory' tab renders SidebarMemory (a compact context/memory view extracted from MemoryPanel), while the standalone MemoryPanel component exists separately. There are now two parallel memory view components with overlapping functionality — SidebarMemory (bottom panel) and MemoryPanel (standalone). MemoryPanel has richer display (individual MemoryItemCard components with full card layout) while SidebarMemory is a simpler compact list.
findings.recommendation: Decide which is canonical. If the bottom panel is the primary location for memory, use MemoryPanel there (it already handles the compact/full distinction via the header Show conditionals). Remove SidebarMemory or make it explicitly a thin wrapper around MemoryPanel.
